symptom_or_topic: Sudden face drooping, facial numbness, or weakness on one side
reasoning: Sudden unilateral facial weakness or numbness is a classic stroke warning sign. Stroke treatment is time-critical; emergency services should be activated immediately.

sources.title: CDC - Signs and Symptoms of Stroke

symptom_or_topic: Sudden arm or leg weakness or numbness, especially on one side
reasoning: Sudden unilateral arm or leg weakness or numbness may indicate acute stroke and requires immediate emergency response.

sources.title: CDC - Signs and Symptoms of Stroke

symptom_or_topic: Sudden confusion, trouble speaking, or difficulty understanding speech
reasoning: Acute speech or comprehension difficulty is a stroke warning sign. Emergency activation is required because delay can reduce eligibility for time-sensitive stroke therapy.

sources.title: CDC - Signs and Symptoms of Stroke

symptom_or_topic: Sudden trouble seeing in one or both eyes
reasoning: Sudden vision loss or visual disturbance can be a stroke symptom and should be treated as a medical emergency.

sources.title: CDC - Signs and Symptoms of Stroke

symptom_or_topic: Sudden trouble walking, dizziness, loss of balance, or loss of coordination
reasoning: Sudden gait disturbance, dizziness, balance loss, or coordination loss may represent acute stroke and requires immediate emergency evaluation.

sources.title: CDC - Signs and Symptoms of Stroke

symptom_or_topic: Sudden severe headache with no known cause
reasoning: A sudden severe headache without known cause can be a stroke warning sign, including possible hemorrhagic stroke.

sources.title: CDC - Signs and Symptoms of Stroke

symptom_or_topic: Severe abdominal pain after trauma, accident, or injury
reasoning: Severe abdominal pain associated with trauma can indicate internal injury or bleeding and needs emergency medical assistance.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Abdominal pain: When to see a doctor

symptom_or_topic: Severe abdominal pain with chest pressure or chest pain
reasoning: Abdominal pain with chest pressure or chest pain may represent cardiac or other life-threatening disease and requires emergency response.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Abdominal pain: When to see a doctor
ACEP - Know When to Go to the ER

symptom_or_topic: Abdominal pain with black stool, bloody stool, vomiting blood, or blood in urine
reasoning: Abdominal pain with evidence of gastrointestinal or urinary bleeding may indicate serious internal bleeding or acute disease requiring emergency evaluation.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Abdominal pain in adults

symptom_or_topic: Abdominal pain with swollen and tender abdomen
reasoning: A swollen, tender abdomen with pain can indicate peritonitis, obstruction, internal bleeding, or other acute abdominal emergency.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Abdominal pain in adults

symptom_or_topic: Abdominal pain with high fever or persistent vomiting
reasoning: Abdominal pain with high fever or persistent vomiting can signal serious infection, obstruction, dehydration, or another condition needing emergency care.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Abdominal pain in adults

symptom_or_topic: Chest pain or upper abdominal pressure lasting two minutes or more
reasoning: Chest or upper abdominal pressure lasting two minutes or more can indicate heart attack or another emergency condition.

sources.title: ACEP - Know When to Go to the ER

symptom_or_topic: Chest pain with nausea, sweating, dizziness, or shortness of breath
reasoning: Chest pain with autonomic symptoms or shortness of breath is concerning for heart attack or other life-threatening cardiopulmonary disease.

sources.title: MedlinePlus - Chest Pain
MedlinePlus - Warning signs and symptoms of heart disease

symptom_or_topic: Difficulty breathing or shortness of breath
reasoning: Difficulty breathing or shortness of breath may reflect serious respiratory, cardiac, allergic, or thromboembolic disease and needs emergency evaluation.

sources.title: ACEP - Know When to Go to the ER

symptom_or_topic: Fainting, sudden dizziness, sudden weakness, or difficulty waking
reasoning: Fainting, sudden dizziness, sudden weakness, or reduced arousability may indicate neurologic, cardiac, metabolic, or other emergency conditions.

sources.title: ACEP - Know When to Go to the ER

symptom_or_topic: Difficult or painful breathing with chest pain or tightness
reasoning: Breathing difficulty with chest pain or tightness can indicate pulmonary embolism or another life-threatening cardiopulmonary emergency.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Blood clots: When to see a doctor

symptom_or_topic: Coughing bloody sputum with fast heartbeat or lightheadedness
reasoning: Coughing blood with tachycardia or lightheadedness may indicate pulmonary embolism or severe cardiopulmonary disease.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Blood clots: When to see a doctor

symptom_or_topic: Arm or leg swelling, redness, warmth, or pain suggestive of DVT without breathing symptoms
reasoning: Possible deep vein thrombosis should be assessed promptly to prevent progression to pulmonary embolism, even if pulmonary symptoms are absent.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Blood clots: When to see a doctor
Mayo Clinic - Deep vein thrombosis: Symptoms and causes

symptom_or_topic: Known or suspected infection that is getting worse with confusion, shortness of breath, extreme pain, clammy skin, fever, shivering, or weak pulse
reasoning: Sepsis is a life-threatening medical emergency; infection plus systemic warning signs requires immediate medical evaluation.

sources.title: CDC - About Sepsis
CDC - Protect Yourself And Your Family From Sepsis

symptom_or_topic: Allergic reaction with throat, face, lip, or tongue swelling; wheezing; breathing difficulty; faintness; or severe multi-system symptoms
reasoning: Signs of anaphylaxis require immediate epinephrine when available and emergency medical services because untreated anaphylaxis can be fatal.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Anaphylaxis: First aid
CDC - Recognizing and Responding to Anaphylaxis
FARE - Recognizing and Responding to a Reaction

symptom_or_topic: Medicare service has an applicable National Coverage Determination
reasoning: A National Coverage Determination is a national Medicare coverage rule. If an NCD exists, it controls whether Medicare covers the item or service nationally.

sources.title: CMS - Medicare Coverage Determination Process
CMS - MCD Search

symptom_or_topic: Medicare service has no applicable NCD but may be governed by MAC policy
reasoning: A Local Coverage Determination is a Medicare Administrative Contractor determination about whether an item or service is covered on a contractor-wide basis.

sources.title: CMS - Local Coverage Determinations
CMS - Medicare Program Integrity Manual, Chapter 13

symptom_or_topic: Medicare item or service covered only under Coverage with Evidence Development
reasoning: CMS may cover certain items or services only in the context of a clinical study when an NCD requires Coverage with Evidence Development.

sources.title: CMS - Coverage with Evidence Development

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department blepharoplasty, blepharoptosis repair, or brow ptosis repair
reasoning: CMS requires prior authorization for specified hospital outpatient department eyelid and brow procedures to confirm medical necessity before payment.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services
CMS - Final List of Outpatient Department Services That Require Prior Authorization

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department botulinum toxin injections
reasoning: CMS lists botulinum toxin injections among hospital outpatient department services requiring prior authorization.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department panniculectomy
reasoning: CMS lists panniculectomy among hospital outpatient department services requiring prior authorization.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department rhinoplasty
reasoning: CMS lists rhinoplasty among hospital outpatient department services requiring prior authorization.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department vein ablation
reasoning: CMS lists vein ablation among hospital outpatient department services requiring prior authorization.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department implanted spinal neurostimulator
reasoning: CMS added implanted spinal neurostimulators to the hospital outpatient department prior authorization program for service dates on or after July 1, 2021.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department cervical fusion with disc removal
reasoning: CMS added cervical fusion with disc removal to the hospital outpatient department prior authorization program for service dates on or after July 1, 2021.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services

symptom_or_topic: Hospital outpatient department facet joint interventions
reasoning: CMS added facet joint interventions to the hospital outpatient department prior authorization program for service dates on or after July 1, 2023.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services

symptom_or_topic: Medicare home health agency selected pre-claim review under Review Choice Demonstration
reasoning: Under the Review Choice Demonstration, home health agencies may select pre-claim review; services can begin before review, but review must occur before final claim submission.

sources.title: CMS - Review Choice Demonstration for Home Health Services
CMS - Review Choice Demonstration FAQs

symptom_or_topic: Repetitive scheduled non-emergent ambulance transport under Medicare
reasoning: CMS prior authorization applies to repetitive scheduled non-emergent ambulance transport to verify compliance with Medicare coverage, coding, and payment rules before services are rendered and claims submitted.

sources.title: CMS - Prior Authorization of Repetitive, Scheduled Non-Emergent Ambulance Transport

symptom_or_topic: Medicare preventive colorectal cancer screening colonoscopy or stool-based screening with follow-up screening colonoscopy
reasoning: CMS guidance states deductible and coinsurance do not apply to covered non-invasive stool-based colorectal screening tests or qualifying follow-up screening colonoscopy.

sources.title: CMS - Updates to Colorectal Cancer Screening Policies
CMS - Medicare Claims Processing Manual Change Request R13248CP

symptom_or_topic: Medicare preventive and screening services
reasoning: Medicare covers many preventive services, including exams, shots, lab tests, screenings, monitoring, counseling, and education; coverage frequency and cost-sharing depend on the specific service.

sources.title: Medicare.gov - Preventive and screening services

symptom_or_topic: Average-risk adults age 45 to 75 years
reasoning: USPSTF recommends colorectal cancer screening for adults age 45 to 75 years.

sources.title: USPSTF - Colorectal Cancer: Screening
CDC - Screening for Colorectal Cancer

symptom_or_topic: Adults age 76 to 85 years considering colorectal cancer screening
reasoning: USPSTF recommends selective colorectal cancer screening from age 76 to 85 based on overall health, prior screening history, and patient preferences.

sources.title: USPSTF - Colorectal Cancer: Screening
CDC - Screening for Colorectal Cancer

symptom_or_topic: People at increased risk for colorectal cancer
reasoning: People with increased colorectal cancer risk should discuss earlier start age, test choice, and screening interval with a clinician rather than following only average-risk rules.

sources.title: CDC - Screening for Colorectal Cancer

symptom_or_topic: Women age 40 to 74 years at average risk for breast cancer
reasoning: USPSTF recommends biennial screening mammography for women age 40 to 74 years.

sources.title: USPSTF - Breast Cancer: Screening
CDC - Screening for Breast Cancer

symptom_or_topic: Women age 75 years or older considering breast cancer screening
reasoning: USPSTF finds evidence insufficient for routine mammography screening in women age 75 or older; individualized clinician discussion is appropriate.

sources.title: USPSTF - Breast Cancer: Screening

symptom_or_topic: Women age 21 to 29 years with cervix
reasoning: USPSTF recommends cervical cancer screening every 3 years with cervical cytology alone for women age 21 to 29 years.

sources.title: USPSTF - Cervical Cancer: Screening

symptom_or_topic: Women age 30 to 65 years with cervix
reasoning: USPSTF recommends cervical cancer screening every 3 years with cytology alone, every 5 years with high-risk HPV testing alone, or every 5 years with cotesting.

sources.title: USPSTF - Cervical Cancer: Screening

symptom_or_topic: Adults age 19 years or older: annual influenza vaccination
reasoning: CDC adult immunization schedule is used to determine age-based adult vaccine recommendations, including routine annual influenza vaccination for adults.

sources.title: CDC - Adult Immunization Schedule by Age
CDC - Recommended Adult Immunization Schedule

symptom_or_topic: Adults age 50 years or older: recombinant zoster vaccine
reasoning: CDC adult immunization schedule includes age-based shingles vaccination recommendations for adults beginning at age 50.

sources.title: CDC - Adult Immunization Schedule by Age
CDC - Recommended Adult Immunization Schedule

symptom_or_topic: Adults age 50 years or older: pneumococcal vaccination status review
reasoning: CDC adult immunization schedule should be used to determine whether pneumococcal vaccination is indicated based on age, prior vaccine history, and risk factors.

sources.title: CDC - Adult Immunization Schedule by Age
CDC - Recommended Adult Immunization Schedule

symptom_or_topic: Metformin use with eGFR below 30 mL/min/1.73 m2
reasoning: Metformin is contraindicated when eGFR is below 30 mL/min/1.73 m2 because drug accumulation increases risk of metformin-associated lactic acidosis.

sources.title: StatPearls - Metformin
MedlinePlus - Metformin

symptom_or_topic: Metformin use in hepatic impairment or hepatic failure
reasoning: Metformin should generally be avoided in hepatic impairment because impaired lactate clearance can increase lactic acidosis risk.

sources.title: StatPearls - Metformin
Mayo Clinic - Metformin revisited

symptom_or_topic: Metformin use with iodinated contrast in eGFR 30-60, hepatic impairment, alcoholism, heart failure, or intra-arterial contrast
reasoning: FDA-labeled practice summarized by pharmacy guidance recommends discontinuing metformin before or at contrast administration in higher-risk patients and rechecking eGFR after 48 hours before restarting if renal function is stable.

sources.title: University of Illinois Chicago Drug Information Group - Best practices on holding metformin prior to and post contrast administration
StatPearls - Contrast-Induced Nephropathy
MedlinePlus - CT angiography head and neck

symptom_or_topic: Metformin use with excessive alcohol intake or binge drinking
reasoning: Excess alcohol can increase risk of lactic acidosis and hypoglycemia in patients taking metformin; patients should avoid excessive alcohol and seek clinician guidance.

sources.title: MedlinePlus - Metformin
Drugs.com - Metformin and Alcohol/Food Interactions

symptom_or_topic: Metformin use during acute hypoxia, shock, sepsis, circulatory failure, myocardial infarction, or severe dehydration
reasoning: Acute hypoxia, hypoperfusion, sepsis, shock, and severe dehydration increase risk of metformin-associated lactic acidosis and warrant urgent clinician review or temporary withholding.

sources.title: Mayo Clinic - Metformin revisited
StatPearls - Metformin-Associated Lactic Acidosis
